content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Donna R. Betancourt 
Acting City Clerk 
City of Santa Monica 
1685 Main Street 
Santa Monica, CA 
90401-3295 
Dear Ms. Betancourt: 
June 16, 1988 
RE: 
Your request for advice 
Our File No. A-88-208 
You have requested advice regarding the campaign provisions 
of the Political Reform Act of 1974.11 
QUESTION 
Maya late contribution report be sent by electronic mail? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes, assuming the filing officer who receives the 
electronically sent late contribution report is willing to make 
a hardcopy of the report and place it in the file of the 
candidate or committee which sent it. 
FACTS 
As Acting city Clerk for the City of Santa Monica, you 
received a late contribution report via electronic mail. 
The 
report was received within 24 hours after the contribution was 
received by the filer and contained all the required 
information. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 84203(a) requires each candidate or committee that 
makes or receives a late contribution, as defined in Section 
82036, to report the late contribution to each office with 
which the candidate or committee is required to file its next 
campaign statement pursuant to Section 84215. 
1JGovernment Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code 
of Regulations Section 18000, et seg. 
All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code 
of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


Betancourt, Donna 
Page Two 
Section 84203(b) states that a late contribution must be 
reported by mailgram, telegram, guaranteed overnight mail 
through the United states Postal Service, or personal 
delivery. 
It must be reported within 24 hours of the time it 
is made in the case of the candidate or committee that makes 
the contribution and within 24 hours of the time it is received 
in the case of the recipient. 
Although the law specifies the manner in which a late 
contribution report must be sent, the intent of section 
84203(b) is met by electronic mail. 
The fact that electronic 
mail is not widely used or recognized does not negate the fact 
that all the information required by section 84203(a) is 
provided in a timely manner by electronic mail. 
However, since this information must be made available to 
the general public, a hardcopy must be made immediately and 
placed in the filer's file. 
Therefore, the filing officer who 
receives the report must be willing to make a hardcopy. 
If you have any additional questions, please call me at 
(916) 322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths

Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Donna R. Betancourt 
Acting city Clerk 
1685 Main street 
santa Monica, CA 
90401-3295 
Dear Ms. Betancourt: 
June 2, 1988 
Re: 
88-208 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on June 2, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try. to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
• 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to the information needed. 
If your request is 
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we 
can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. 
Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours,

MONICA

I would appreciate recelvlng a ruling regarding the legality of 
filing a late contribution report by electronic mail. 
The FPPC 
direction reads, 
"Late contribution reports must be filed by 
telegram, mailgram, 
guaranteed overnight mail or by personal 
delivery 
within 
24 
hours 
of 
making 
or 
receiving 
the 
contribution." 
Please advise whether a late contribution report 
filed with the city Clerk via electronic mail fulfills this 
filing obligation. 
(Please note that I made this request for a ruling by telephone 
on this date, and was advised that filing by electronic mail is 
an acceptable 
form of "personal delivery," provided that a 
hardopy is made of the electronic memo and is placed in the 
candidate's file. 
I was asked by the FPPC to make the request 
for a ruling by letter.)
